stories.story: The FBI charged Keonna Thomas, a 30-year-old Philadelphia woman, on Thursday with trying to travel overseas to fight for ISIS. An FBI complaint cites numerous social media messages dating back to August 2013 that were sent by Keonna Thomas, also known as "Young Lioness" and "Fatayat Al Khilafah." One Twitter message from Keonna Thomas said, "If we truly knew the realities ... we all would be rushing to join our brothers in the front lines pray ALLAH accept us as shuhada [martyrs]." The FBI said Keonna Thomas purchased an electronic visa to Turkey on March 23, and on March 26, Keonna Thomas purchased a ticket to Barcelona with a March 29 departure. Keonna Thomas was charged with knowingly attempting to provide material support and resources to a designated foreign terrorist organization.
key_facts: FBI charged Keonna Thomas, 30, Philadelphia woman, Thursday
Keonna Thomas tried to travel overseas to fight for ISIS
Social media messages from Keonna Thomas date back to August 2013
Keonna Thomas known as "Young Lioness" and "Fatayat Al Khilafah"
Keonna Thomas purchased e-visa to Turkey on March 23
Keonna Thomas purchased ticket to Barcelona on March 26 for March 29 departure
Keonna Thomas charged with providing material support to a foreign terrorist organization
explicit_unknowns: It's not clear when or where Keonna Thomas was arrested

stories.story: Keonna Thomas, a 30-year-old Philadelphia woman, is one of three women arrested this week on terror charges. The FBI charged Keonna Thomas on Thursday with trying to travel overseas to fight for ISIS. Two New York women, Noelle Velentzas, 28, and her former roommate, Asia Siddiqui, 31, were also taken into custody on Thursday. Noelle Velentzas, 28, and Asia Siddiqui, 31, were arrested in New York and accused of planning to build an explosive device for attacks in the United States, federal prosecutors said.

FBI charged Keonna Thomas with trying to travel overseas to fight for ISIS
Noelle Velentzas, 28, New York woman, and Asia Siddiqui, 31, New York woman, arrested Thursday
Noelle Velentzas and Asia Siddiqui accused of planning to build an explosive device for attacks in the United States
explicit_unknowns: It's not clear when or where Keonna Thomas was arrested

stories.story: On Thursday, Noelle Velentzas, 28, and her former roommate, Asia Siddiqui, 31, were arrested in New York. Federal prosecutors accused Noelle Velentzas, 28, and Asia Siddiqui, 31, of planning to build an explosive device for attacks in the United States. These two New York women were taken into custody on terror charges this week, alongside Keonna Thomas, a 30-year-old Philadelphia woman. The FBI charged Keonna Thomas with trying to travel overseas to fight for ISIS.

stories.story: The FBI said Keonna Thomas, a 30-year-old Philadelphia woman, purchased an electronic visa to Turkey on March 23, and a ticket to Barcelona on March 26, with a March 29 departure. Turkey is known as the easiest place from which to enter Syria and join ISIS. An ISIS manual advises recruits to buy round-trip tickets to vacation spots such as Spain and then purchase tickets for their real destination once they arrive overseas, the FBI said. Keonna Thomas, also known as "Young Lioness" and "Fatayat Al Khilafah," had a March 29 departure and an April 15 return to the United States on her Barcelona ticket.

stories.story: In the past 18 months, the Justice Department's National Security Division has prosecuted or is prosecuting more than 30 cases of people attempting to travel abroad to join or provide support to terrorist groups. Of those cases, 18 allegedly involve support to ISIS. Homeland Security Secretary Jeh Johnson told reporters Thursday that "The terrorist threat is more decentralized, more diffuse, more complicated" and "involves the potential lone wolf actor, it involves the effective use of social media, the Internet." Keonna Thomas, a 30-year-old Philadelphia woman, was charged by the FBI with trying to travel overseas to fight for ISIS, and her case involves social media messages.

Keonna Thomas's case involves social media messages
